senses.definition: used to suggest that  patience can provide comfort and relief during difficult times, and can help to heal emotional wounds and problems

examples.x: When I'm feeling stressed or overwhelmed, I try to remember that patience is a plaster for all sores, and that taking a step back and giving myself time can help to alleviate my anxiety.

word_list.word: a handful of patience is worth more than a bushel of brains

senses.definition: used to imply that patience is more important than intelligence or knowledge, and can often lead to greater success in navigating challenges and achieving goals

word_list.word: good things come to those who wait

senses.definition: used to imply that if one is patient and persistent, they will eventually be rewarded with success or good things

examples.x: He remained optimistic and confident despite facing multiple rejections from publishers, trusting that good things come to those who wait.

word_list.word: it will be all the same a hundred years hence
word_list.pronunciation: …™t w…™l biňź ňą…Ēňźl √į…ô sňąe…™m …ź hňą Ćnd…Ļ…ôd jňą…™…Ļz hňą…õns

senses.definition: used to suggest that the passing of time can put things in perspective, and that the things that seem important or significant in the present may not matter in the long run

examples.x: I try not to get too caught up in the small things, because in the grand scheme of things, it will be all the same a hundred years hence.

word_list.word: all things are difficult before they are easy

senses.definition: used to suggest that initial difficulties are a natural part of the learning process and with perseverance and determination, tasks can become easier to handle

examples.x: When she first started learning how to play the guitar, it was difficult and frustrating, but with regular practice, the chords became easier to play, proving that all things are difficult before they are easy.

senses.definition: used to advise one to be patient and wait until one has achieved actual results before expecting or assuming success and enjoying the rewards

word_list.word: long foretold, long last; short notice, soon past

senses.definition: used to imply that events that are planned for a long time tend to have a lasting impact, while those that are hastily done tend to be short-lived.
used to imply that being able to wait calmly and persistently without becoming frustrated, angry, or anxious is a valuable and desirable trait that can ultimately lead to success

examples.x: When faced with a difficult situation, I try to remember that patience is a virtue and that taking a step back and waiting can often lead to better outcomes.

word_list.pronunciation: sňą…Ēft slňąo ä fňąa…™…ö mňĆe…™ks swňąiňźt mňą…Ďňźlt

senses.definition: used to imply that taking the time to do something right and with patience will yield better results than rushing through it

examples.x: The baker always took his time and followed the recipe carefully, believing that soft fire makes sweet malt, and his customers appreciated the delicious and perfectly baked bread.

word_list.pronunciation: sňą…Ēftli sňą…Ēftli kňą√¶t Éiňź mňą Ćnki

senses.definition: used to imply that a cautious, patient, and persistent approach is often more effective in achieving one's goals than a hasty, aggressive, or reckless approach
used to imply that taking breaks and having leisure time can lead to positive outcomes or opportunities that may not have been possible with constant work

examples.x: After months of working long hours and neglecting his personal life, John took a weekend off to go hiking and ended up meeting a business partner who would later help him launch his own company; it just goes to show that there is luck in leisure.

senses.definition: used to suggest that the passage of time can lessen the intensity of emotional pain or suffering caused by a difficult experience or situation, and eventually lead to healing and recovery

examples.x: After going through a painful breakup, I thought I would never be able to love again, but time heals all wounds, and I eventually moved on.

senses.definition: used to imply that the passage of time can bring about positive changes, personal growth, and achievement in life, even in seemingly impossible situations, if one remains patient and perseveres

examples.x: After the accident, I was in a lot of pain, but the doctor reassured me that time works wonders and that I would heal.

word_list.pronunciation: ňąe…™p…Ļ…ôl  Éňąa ä…öz b…Ļňą…™Ňč mňąe…™ flňąa ä…öz

senses.definition: used to suggest that enduring difficult or challenging times can lead to positive outcomes and rewards

examples.x: Even though the project was difficult and challenging, we knew that our hard work would pay off in the end, just like April showers bring May flowers.

senses.definition: used to suggest that constantly monitoring or worrying about something can make it seem like it is taking longer to happen

senses.definition: used to imply that if one waits long enough, justice will eventually be served and one's enemies will face the consequences of their actions

word_list.word: patience is bitter but its fruit is sweet
word_list.pronunciation: pňąe…™ É…ôns …™z bňą…™…ĺ…ö bňĆ Ćt …™ts f…Ļňąuňźt …™z swňąiňźt

senses.definition: used to suggest that waiting for something can be difficult, but the end result is often rewarding, emphasizing the importance of being patient and persistent in pursuing one's goals

examples.x: When I feel like giving up, I remind myself that patience is bitter but its fruit is sweet - if I keep working hard, I'll eventually see the results I want.
